title: What is the significance of
wikitext:
[]: What is the significance of [spoilers]?             Quote Post by CyberXIII » Thu Oct 15, 2009 10:52 pm ..the halos? Every Angel has shown one so far, and Eva-01 pulls one in the end of 2.0. What do we know about them?

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Fri Oct 16, 2009 12:12 am They're Angels. Angels have Halos. Voila. As for what they actually are, it seems to be that they're unfolding manifestations of the AT Field or something.

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Fri Oct 16, 2009 5:32 am Anno and friends think they are cool. Seriously, even the cross shaped explosions. The actual quote is around somewhere.

[]: Re: What is the significance of...(spoilers)             Quote Post by SaltyJoe » Fri Oct 16, 2009 9:48 am CyberXIII wrote: What do we know about them?

[]:             Quote Post by Ryjin » Sun Oct 18, 2009 11:29 am If I were to wager a guess it would be that it's caused by stressing the AT field with a lot of power to allow a non flying angel so gain some form of flight capacity (I would also guess that flying units have a design that allows them to use there field to fly more efficiently hence why they don't have one) and to fanwank why the halo appears I can only guess the AT field has effects similar to that of a electromagnetic field and when an angel "jumps" the field spins causing trapped particles to migrate to the top of the field where they get accelerated into cyclotron radiation forming the halo effect we see. IT'S SCIENCE I SAY, SCIENCE!

[]: Re: What is the significance of...(spoilers)             Quote Post by Boredom » Mon Oct 19, 2009 3:11 pm CyberXIII wrote: ..the halos? Every Angel has shown one so far, and Eva-01 pulls one in the end of 2.0. What do we know about them?

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Mon Oct 19, 2009 3:15 pm Didn't someone say, I think it was Sadamato, they the used religious stuff because they thought it looked cool?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Oct 19, 2009 3:24 pm I...don't remember ALL the Angels in Rebuild so far having halos (eg: Sachiel, Clocky, and Tunnely). Did they and I'm just forgetting?

[]:             Quote Post by schismatics » Mon Oct 19, 2009 3:32 pm ^ Tunniel kinda had one when he curled up and floated up.

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Mon Oct 19, 2009 3:36 pm Clockiel didn't have a halo and I don't think Ramiel or Zuruel had one either.

[]:             Quote Post by CyberXIII » Mon Oct 19, 2009 6:10 pm Ramiel had a sideways one when he was charging his new wave motion gun. Zeruel....no, he didn't have one.

[]:             Quote Post by Ryjin » Mon Oct 19, 2009 7:54 pm No none of the flying ones had a halo, only ones who don't get one when when they fly or super jump. As I said in my above post, I think it has to do with them stressing there AT Field, likely the result of cyclotron radiation (though to be perfectly honest the halo should be around there waist more like a hula hoop if this is the case, unless there AT Field is project from where there crown chakra should be).

[]:             Quote Post by Janrok24 » Mon Oct 19, 2009 9:18 pm Eh, its probably because they look cool.

[]:             Quote Post by EvaCub » Tue Oct 20, 2009 1:51 am Joseph the PRPD wrote: Clockiel didn't have a halo and I don't think Ramiel or Zuruel had one either.

[]:             Quote Post by Teague » Tue Oct 20, 2009 4:36 am I'm willing to bet it has something to do with the S2 engine...or it's equivalent if it doesn't exist in Rebuild... has the S2 engine been mentioned by name, come to think of it?

[]:             Quote Post by EdZ » Tue Oct 20, 2009 7:29 am Ramiel had a halo when he first appeared. I couldn't spot Clockiel's, but that's probably because of the poor video quality. Unit 01 also picked one up before delivering Zeruel's beatdown. As to why: Either it's the equivalent visual indicator of "this thing ahs an S2 engine", or simply because it's a nice visual theme. Probably the latter.

[]:             Quote Post by Rodeo » Tue Oct 20, 2009 10:00 am Beyond looking cool it's probably supposed to be a visual representation telling us that the AT magic that is currently being employed is of an angelically high power-level.

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Tue Oct 20, 2009 1:43 pm Teague wrote: I'm willing to bet it has something to do with the S2 engine...or it's equivalent if it doesn't exist in Rebuild... has the S2 engine been mentioned by name, come to think of it?
